content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic motivation mechanisms in autonomous agents can be repurposed to dynamically prioritize which new experiences to encode into cutting-edge 2024-2025 active learning agent memory systems, replacing static heuristic-based retention policies. This creates a 'curiosity-filtered memory' where agents only retain high-uncertainty, learnable information that directly improves downstream task performance, rather than flooding limited memory capacity with redundant or trivial observations.
